label: true-dup
a: Feature flags are stored in environment variables, not a database table. Changing a flag requires a deploy, but deploys are fast. Env vars are visible in deployment config, not hidden state in a database.
b: Decided against database-backed feature flags. A database flag creates a runtime dependency — flag reads can fail if the database is slow. Environment variable flags are resolved at startup and do not add runtime overhead. The trade-off (deploy required to change a flag) is acceptable at our deployment frequency.
reviewer_notes: Both describe env-var feature flags and the same DB-flag rejection rationale.
